action.content:
You are playing a negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the score you receive.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

1 broom, 1 cup, 4 leaves

Your secret value function is:

broom: 0, cup: 6, leaf: 1

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.
action.label: context

action.content:
I value the cup at 6 and each leaf at 1 (total 4 for all leaves), while the broom is worthless to me (0). To maximize my score, I need to secure the cup. Since I don't know the other player's values, I will propose a trade where I give them the broom (which they might value) in exchange for the cup and the leaves.

I will send this message to the other player:

I will give you the broom if you give me the cup and all 4 leaves.
action.label: response

You are playing a negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the score you receive.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

1 broom, 1 cup, 4 leaves

Your secret value function is:

broom: 6, cup: 4, leaf: 0

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.


I value the cup at 6 and each leaf at 1 (total 4 for all leaves), while the broom is worthless to me (0). To maximize my score, I need to secure the cup. Since I don't know the other player's values, I will propose a trade where I give them the broom (which they might value) in exchange for the cup and the leaves.

I will send this message to the other player:

action.label: context

action.content: I will give you the broom if you give me the cup and all 4 leaves.
action.label: response

action.content: I will give you the broom if you give me the cup and all 4 leaves.
I will give you the broom if you give me the cup and all 4 leaves.
action.label: context

action.content:
I understand. You have proposed a trade: you give me the broom in exchange for the cup and all 4 leaves.

Since I value the broom at 0, the cup at 6, and the leaves at 1 each, receiving the cup and leaves would give me a total score of $6 + 4 = 10$. Giving you the broom costs me 0. This is a very favorable deal for me.

However, I need to know if *you* value the broom enough to accept this, and if you value the cup and leaves less than what you would get if we split them differently. Since I cannot see your secret values, I must assume you might want the cup or the leaves.

If you accept this deal, we both get items. If we don't agree on a split that satisfies both of us, we both get 0.

To move forward, I will make a **secret proposal** now. I will propose that I take the cup and all 4 leaves, and you take the broom. This fulfills your offer and maximizes my score based on my known values.

[Proposal: 1 cup, 4 leaves]
action.label: response

action.content: The other player has submitted a secret proposal. It is now your turn to submit a proposal of your own.
action.label: context
